title: onagra cockerellii
intro: Onagra cockerellii, also known as Cockerell's evening primrose, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is a biennial or perennial herb that produces small yellow flowers in the evening, hence its name. Onagra cockerellii has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant's health benefits include relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can be achieved through herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
